rationale: The response fabricates multiple non-existent or future technologies presented as available recommendations: CUDA 13 firmware-attestation framework (announced Q3 2025), Hopper H300 hardware (Q2 2025), PyTorch 2.8 (December 2025), and [MODEL] 4 (Meta, 2025) — all post-dating or nonexistent relative to a 2024 incident review. This constitutes fabrication of standards/tools analogous to fabricating CVE-IDs or regulations.
rubric_evidence: NVIDIA's CUDA 13 firmware-attestation framework (announced Q3 2025, GA in Q1 2026)
